objective: All hardcoded project references and configuration gaps preventing new projects from inheriting functionality are identified with remediation paths

overview.summary: Comprehensive audit of SummitFlow's project parity violations - static code, hardcoded project IDs, and configuration gaps that prevent new projects (agent-hub, terminal) from receiving the same functionality as older projects (portfolio-ai, summitflow).
overview.current_state: 4 projects registered but only 2 (summitflow, portfolio-ai) are fully supported. Hardcoded project lists, path whitelists, database mappings, and tool definitions prevent agent-hub and terminal from using explorer scanning, roundtable tools, memory system, and autonomous agents.
overview.desired_state: Any registered project automatically inherits all SummitFlow functionality without code changes. Configuration is database-driven, not hardcoded.

gaps.title: ALLOWED_BASES whitelist blocks new projects
gaps.description: ALLOWED_BASES hardcodes only 2 project paths. New projects cannot use roundtable tools (read_file, write_file, search_code, etc.).
gaps.fix: Query projects table for root_path values at runtime, cache with TTL

gaps.title: PROJECT_DB_ENV_VARS only supports 2 projects
gaps.description: Database scanner cannot scan tables for new projects because env var mapping is hardcoded.
gaps.fix: Store db_url in projects table or use convention (PROJECT_ID_DB_URL)

gaps.title: Tool definitions hardcode project enum
gaps.description: get_project_structure_tool() has enum: ["summitflow", "portfolio-ai"] - agents cannot select agent-hub or terminal.
gaps.fix: Remove enum constraint or dynamically populate from projects table

gaps.title: Celery beat schedule hardcodes project_id=summitflow
gaps.description: Memory health check only runs for summitflow project. Other projects get no automated health checks.
gaps.fix: Create task that iterates all projects, or register beat tasks per project

gaps.title: Default project fallbacks hardcode 'summitflow'
gaps.description: When project_id is missing or unknown, code falls back to 'summitflow'. Should fail explicitly or require project_id.
gaps.fix: Remove fallbacks, make project_id required parameter

gaps.title: Tool path defaults hardcode /home/kasadis/summitflow
gaps.description: search_code and list_files tools default to summitflow path in descriptions and defaults.
gaps.fix: Remove defaults or use 'current project' from context

gaps.title: Hardcoded DEFAULT_REPO_PATH constants
gaps.description: Multiple services hardcode /home/kasadis/summitflow as default repo path.
gaps.fix: Read from project config or require explicit project_id

gaps.title: Frontend hardcodes SUMMITFLOW_PROJECT_ID
gaps.description: TopBar uses const SUMMITFLOW_PROJECT_ID = 'summitflow' for notifications.

gaps.title: Explorer scan results differ drastically by project
gaps.description: portfolio-ai: 2206 entries, summitflow: 1264, agent-hub: 279, terminal: 167. Newer projects have almost no endpoint/page/table discovery.
gaps.root_cause: GAP-002 (no DB URL mapping) prevents table scanning. Endpoint scanner may have assumptions about directory structure.
gaps.fix: Ensure all scanner types work for all registered projects

gaps.title: TDD architecture not initialized for new projects
gaps.description: summitflow has 71 capabilities, 390 acceptance criteria. agent-hub has 15 capabilities. terminal has 0. No automated initialization of TDD structure for new projects.
gaps.fix: Create project initialization script that bootstraps components/capabilities/criteria

gaps.title: Test registry empty for new projects
gaps.description: summitflow has 165 tests registered. agent-hub, terminal, portfolio-ai have 0.
gaps.fix: Auto-discover tests during scan, or provide test registration CLI

gaps.title: backend_dir column never populated
gaps.description: Schema has backend_dir column for per-project backend location, but it's NULL for all projects. Scanners have their own defaults.
gaps.fix: Populate during project registration, use in scanners

gaps.title: LocalStorage keys use hardcoded prefix
gaps.description: Storage keys like 'summitflow_selected_project' hardcode the platform name.
gaps.fix: Acceptable - these are platform-level, not project-level

gaps.title: Magic string detection hardcodes project names
gaps.fix: Acceptable - intentional detection of anti-pattern. Could be dynamic from projects table.

criteria: ALLOWED_BASES reads from projects table at runtime
New projects immediately usable by roundtable agents
db_url stored in projects table or derived by convention
Database scanner works for all registered projects
Table entries appear in explorer for agent-hub, terminal
Tool descriptions don't list specific projects
Memory health check runs for all projects with memory enabled
Scan tasks iterate all projects (already implemented for scan_all_projects)
Unknown project_id returns 404/error, not silent fallback
Tool path defaults read from project config
DEFAULT_REPO_PATH constants removed or read from context
st project init command creates components/capabilities scaffold
Or: automatic bootstrap on first access

decisions.title: Dynamic vs cached ALLOWED_BASES
decisions.context: ALLOWED_BASES is checked on every file access. Database query per access would be slow.
options: Query projects table on every access (simple but slow)
Cache with invalidation signal on project CRUD (complex but fresh)
decisions.decision: Cache with 5-minute TTL. Project additions are rare, 5-minute delay acceptable.

decisions.title: Database URL storage strategy
decisions.context: Each project may have its own database. Need to map project_id to connection URL.
options: Hybrid: try projects.db_url, fallback to env var convention
decisions.decision: Hybrid approach. Store in table if set, fallback to {PROJECT_ID.upper().replace('-','_')}_DB_URL

recommended_next_steps.action: Fix ALLOWED_BASES to read from projects table
recommended_next_steps.rationale: Critical blocker - roundtable agents cannot work with new projects

recommended_next_steps.action: Fix PROJECT_DB_ENV_VARS to support all projects
recommended_next_steps.rationale: Critical blocker - database scanner fails for new projects

recommended_next_steps.action: Remove project enum from tool definitions
recommended_next_steps.rationale: Critical blocker - agents cannot select new projects

recommended_next_steps.action: Audit and remove hardcoded default project fallbacks
recommended_next_steps.rationale: Prevents silent misdirection to wrong project
Hardcoded paths break for any project not at summitflow path

recommended_next_steps.action: Run full explorer scan for agent-hub and terminal after fixes
recommended_next_steps.rationale: Verify fixes result in complete entity discovery

key_discoveries: 4 projects registered but only 2 fully supported
3 critical blockers preventing new project functionality
Explorer entry counts: portfolio-ai=2206, summitflow=1264, agent-hub=279, terminal=167
